title: 10 best herbal tinctures for inflamed gums
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for inflamed gums are a natural remedy that combines the power of plants to soothe and heal irritated gum tissues. These concentrated liquids extract the active compounds from herbs like calendula, chamomile, and peppermint, which have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the gums. By using herbal tinctures for inflamed gums, individuals can experience relief from discomfort and improve their oral health, allowing them to enjoy daily activities such as eating and socializing without worry.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as calyxin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, while also inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can cause gum disease. Additionally, pot marigold's antiseptic properties help to promote a healthy oral environment by preventing infection and reducing inflammation, ultimately leading to healthier, happier gums.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Marigold's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and tissue damage in the mouth, reducing swelling and discomfort in inflamed gums.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as lutein and zeaxanthin, reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for gingivitis and periodontal disease.

Antimicrobial properties: Marigold's antimicrobial compounds inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections and inflammation, promoting healthy oral flora and preventing further inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and vitamins, the roots of pot marigold have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums.
Buds: The buds of the pot marigold plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties to combat gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers and clean them thoroughly to remove any debris or impurities.
Combine one cup of dried calendula flowers with two cups of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily, to extract the active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Label and store the final tincture in a cool dark place for up to two years, using 30-40 drops diluted with water as directed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The herb's active compounds, such as echinacin and chicoric acid, have been shown to reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, while also inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, purple coneflower's antimicrobial properties help to eliminate plaque and prevent further bacterial buildup, promoting healthy gums and a reduction in inflammation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Echinacea's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in gum tissue, thereby alleviating swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Echinacea contains bioactive compounds like cichoric acid and echinacein, which exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum inflammation and prevent further irritation.

Immune system support: Purple coneflower tinctures contain immunomodulatory compounds that stimulate the immune system to fight off underlying infections or pathogens contributing to gum inflammation, promoting a healthy balance in the oral cavity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of purple coneflower are rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections associated with gum disease.
Flowers: The flowers contain potent flavonoids and terpenes that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective for treating inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in the late summer or early fall when they are in full bloom.
Clean and dry the harvested materials immediately to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Chop the dried roots and flowers into small pieces and combine them in a clean glass jar.
Combine 1 part echinacea mixture with 5 parts vodka and store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidant-rich extract from sage leaves reduces swelling and soothes irritated gum tissue, providing relief from discomfort and pain. Additionally, sage's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation. By reducing inflammation and eliminating the source of infection, sage tinctures promote healthy gums and a strong dental foundation, ultimately leading to a reduction in symptoms associated with inflamed gums.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Sage tincture's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause gum inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.
Antioxidant activity: Sage's antioxidant properties help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum inflammation and disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, sage roots are often used to create anti-inflammatory tinctures that help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome's antimicrobial properties make it effective against bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Chop 20 grams of fresh leaves into small pieces using scissors or mortise and pestle.
Combine chopped leaves with 200 milliliters of high-proof vodka or rum in a clean glass jar.
Store mixture at room temperature for six weeks, shaking jar daily during that time.
Strain tincture through cheesecloth or coffee filter into dark glass bottle, discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes that contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, licorice tincture's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate gum inflammation. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infection, licorice tincture can provide relief from symptoms such as redness, swelling, and bleeding, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal licorice contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which reduce inflammation in the mouth and alleviate swelling in inflamed gums.

Antimicrobial properties: Licorice extract has been shown to have antimicrobial effects against bacteria and fungi that can cause gum infections and inflammation, helping to prevent further infection and promote healing.

Antioxidant activity: Herbal licorice contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy tissue and reducing the risk of chronic inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain triterpenoid saponins, which help to soothe and calm gum inflammation, while also reducing bacterial growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of glycyrrhiza glabra or purchase dried roots from a reputable supplier weighing approximately two pounds.
Cut the roots into small pieces and combine them with a neutral spirit such as vodka at a ratio of one part roots to four parts spirits.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass container and store it in a cool dark place for four weeks allowing for infusion.
After four weeks strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserving the tincture.
Dilute the tincture with distilled water at a ratio of one part tincture to five parts water and store it in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and antimicrobial properties. The tannins present in maidenhair tree help reduce swelling and ease pain, while the flavonoids work to combat bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, the astringent properties of the tincture help constrict blood vessels, reducing bleeding and promoting healthy gum tissue regeneration. This natural remedy can provide relief from inflamed gums without harsh chemicals or artificial additives.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginkgo biloba contains a variety of flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Maidenhair Tree tinctures can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to gum inflammation and damage, promoting healthy tissue repair and reducing oxidative stress.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's flavonoids can dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the gums and enhancing oxygenation of tissues, which may help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds that help reduce inflammation and fight against bacteria, making them effective in treating inflamed gums.
Bark: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and heal inflamed gum tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted source and clean them thoroughly.
Chop the ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least 6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar discarding solids.
Store the ginkgo biloba tincture in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid and label it properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its soothing properties, which calm inflammation and reduce redness. The mucilage present in slippery elm works as a protective barrier, shielding the gums from irritants and bacteria while promoting healthy tissue repair. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory compounds help to decrease swelling and ease discomfort, providing quick relief for patients suffering from inflamed gums.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm tincture contains mucilages, which are a type of soluble fiber that can coat and protect irritated gum tissue, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in slippery elm tincture help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to gum inflammation and damage, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Slippery Elm contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and protects irritated gum tissues.
Barks: The inner bark of the slippery elm tree contains a high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and discomfort in inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 percent by weight of dried ulmus rubra bark in a clean glass container.
Combine one pint of high-proof vodka with the ulmus rubra bark and allow it to steep for six weeks.
Shake the mixture daily during the steeping process to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container after six weeks.
Transfer the clear tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The mucilages present in marshmallow root work to soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, the antioxidant-rich compounds in marshmallow tincture help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to gum tissue damage. As a result, marshmallow tinctures provide natural relief from inflamed gums, promoting healthy gum tissue and a reduced risk of periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root contains thick, protective mucilages that coat and soothe the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing inflammation and discomfort in inflamed gums.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in marshmallow root help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to gum inflammation and disease.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow root contains various anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that help to reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high mucilage content, which provides anti-inflammatory and soothing properties to alleviate gum inflammation.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing gum inflammation and preventing further infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material using a clean towel or paper towels.
Chop the cleaned and dried plant material into small pieces then measure out 500g.
Combine the chopped plant material with 95% ethanol in a dark glass container and let steep for 14 days.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids before transferring to amber glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tannins present in burdock root have been shown to reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, making it an effective natural remedy for gum inflammation. Additionally, burdock's antibacterial compounds help combat the underlying bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, burdock tinctures can promote healthy gums and alleviate symptoms associated with inflamed gums, such as redness, bleeding, and sensitivity.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and discomfort associated with inflamed gums, providing relief from pain and inflammation.
Antimicrobial: Burdock tincture's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacteria that contribute to gum inflammation and infection, promoting a healthy oral environment.
Antioxidant: Burdock tincture's antioxidant properties can help protect gums from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate gum inflammation and promote tissue breakdown.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, burdock roots have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated gum tissue.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, burdock seeds help to combat bacterial growth and reduce inflammation associated with inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried arctium lappa roots in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Clean and chop the roots into small pieces to release their medicinal properties.
Combine one part chopped arctium lappa roots with two parts high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking daily for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discard the solids and use the resulting tincture as directed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The apigenin in chamomile has been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain, making it an effective remedy for soothing inflamed gums. Additionally, chamomile's antiseptic and antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to gum inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing the risk of further complications. By calming the gums and reducing inflammation, chamomile tinctures can provide relief from discomfort and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, which can contribute to gum disease and swelling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce pain, swelling, and redness associated with inflamed gums, making it an effective natural remedy for oral inflammation.

Antimicrobial properties: Chamomile's antimicrobial agents, including flavonoids and terpenes, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to gum disease and infections, promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant are often used in herbal remedies due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective for soothing inflamed gums.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are a popular ingredient in herbal teas and tinctures, as they contain flavonoids and terpenes that help reduce inflammation and ease gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the early morning after the dew has dried.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers by gently shaking off any debris and spread them out on paper towels.
Combine 250 grams of the clean dried flowers with 500 milliliters of vodka or grain alcohol in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days in a cool dark place with occasional gentle agitation.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The plant-based compounds in elderberry, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, work to reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected area. Additionally, elder tincture's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate gum inflammation. As a natural and effective treatment, elder tinctures can be used as a complementary therapy to traditional dental care, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elder tincture's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to gum inflammation and disease.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in elder tincture have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory activity, reducing swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.
Antimicrobial properties: Elder tincture's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause gum inflammation and disease, such as gingivitis and periodontitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
Barks: The barks of the elder tree contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective against gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers at peak potency for optimal bioactive compound extraction.
Combine 100g of the harvested sambucus nigra flowers with 500ml of vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks allowing for percolation and extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and label with dosage instructions of 20-30 drops diluted in water three times daily for up to 6 weeks.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat inflamed gums with herbal tinctures is by combining a few potent ingredients. Calendula tincture helps reduce inflammation and promotes healing, while Myrrh tincture disinfects the area and prevents infection. Golden Seal tincture soothes and calms the gums, reducing pain and discomfort. Mix equal parts of each tincture in water and swish for 30 seconds, then spit it out before rinsing with warm saltwater. Repeat twice daily for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to inflamed gums that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions characterized by inflammation, pain, and discomfort in the mouth or throat. Examples include aphthous ulcers, oral thrush, and tonsillitis. Herbal tinctures such as Calendula, Echinacea, and Slippery Elm have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healing in these conditions.
